ccc5b2041bb0.statement: Riemann zeta 函数定义为 ζ(s) = Σ(n=1 to ∞) 1/n^s 当 Re(s) > 1，并通过解析延拓扩展到整个复平面（除 s=1 的简单极点）

16df0cdf3482.statement: 临界线定义为 Re(s) = 1/2。Riemann 假设 (RH) 断言所有非平凡零点都在临界线上

9e8f7b0ea02b.statement: 设 ρ_n = 1/2 + iγ_n 为第 n 个零点（按虚部递增排序），其中 0 < γ_1 < γ_2 < γ_3 < ...

7ab7f382a9ce.statement: 零点计数函数 N(T) := #{ρ : 0 < Im(ρ) ≤ T} 满足渐近公式 N(T) ~ (T/2π) log(T/2π) - T/2π

daee8c74477e.statement: 零点的平均归一化间距为 1 / N'(γ_n) = 2π / log(γ_n/2π)，趋近于 2π / log γ_n

3aa5719f3e81.statement: Montgomery pair correlation 猜想：归一化零点间距的统计分布与随机矩阵理论中 Gaussian Unitary Ensemble (GUE) 的本征值间距一致

550efba26079.statement: Odlyzko 计算了高度达 10^13 的零点数据，数值证据强烈支持 Montgomery 猜想

05d046aebda6.statement: 数值观察表明，绝大多数归一化间距 d_n := (γ_{n+1} - γ_n) · log(γ_n)/(2π) 接近 1，但存在罕见的小间距事件

c0eeb0e997ce.statement: 在随机矩阵理论中，GUE 本征值的最小间距服从 Tracy-Widom 分布，这提供了零点间距的概率模型

28831e5bb6e3.statement: 2026年最新结果证明了在 RH 假设下，lim inf (γ_{n+1} - γ_n) / (2π/log γ_n) < 0.50895，突破了此前 0.515 的障碍

a1b66d80048d.statement: 分析 Riemann zeta 函数在临界线上零点的连续间距统计性质，特别是小间距（相对于平均间距）的分布和理论下界。在 RH 假设下，探索是否存在 μ < 1 使得 lim inf (γ_{n+1} - γ_n) / (2π/log γ_n) < μ，以及该常数的最优值。

5cc6b3fdb20b.statement: Under RH, the n-th zero satisfies ρ_n = 1/2 + iγ_n with asymptotic density N'(t) ~ (1/2π)log(t/2π)

dbc8a2b3be38.statement: The pair correlation function R₂(α) = lim_{T→∞} (1/N(T)) Σ_{0<γ,γ'≤T, γ≠γ'} w((γ-γ')·log T/(2π) - α) satisfies R₂(α) = 1 - (sin πα/πα)² for |α| < 1 (Montgomery-Odlyzko)

bd6367651b62.statement: The three-point correlation function R₃(α,β) measuring density of triples (γ_n, γ_n + α·2π/log γ_n, γ_n + β·2π/log γ_n) satisfies a cubic determinant formula when α,β are in the scaling regime |α|,|β| ≪ log log T

6aeaac4be8c2.statement: For the explicit formula, if all spacings satisfied d_n ≥ μ·2π/log γ_n, then for any test function φ with support in [μ, 2μ], ∫_μ^{2μ} R₂(α)φ(α)dα ≥ (∫_μ^{2μ} φ(α)dα) · inf_{α∈[μ,2μ]} R₂(α)

7110b2fad614.statement: Selberg's central limit theorem for log ζ shows that for k-point correlations with k ≥ 4, Var(Σ_{|γ_n - T| ≤ N'(T)·α} 1) = α·N'(T)·(1 + o(1))

0651e8c38ece.statement: The existing proven result shows μ < 0.50895

steps: Assume for contradiction that lim inf d_n ≥ μ for some μ
Then for all large n, the normalized spacing d_n = (γ_{n+1} - γ_n)·log γ_n/(2π) ≥ μ - ε for arbitrarily small ε > 0
Consider three consecutive zeros γ_n, γ_{n+1}, γ_{n+2}. Both gaps satisfy the bound, so γ_{n+1} - γ_n ≥ (μ-ε)·2π/log γ_n and γ_{n+2} - γ_{n+1} ≥ (μ-ε)·2π/log γ_{n+1}
In the normalized coordinates, this means we exclude the region {(α,β) : 0 < α < μ or μ < β < α+μ} from the support of the three-point correlation
Compute the expected count of such configurations. The three-point function integrated over the forbidden region gives I_forbidden = ∫∫_{forbidden} R₃(α,β) dα dβ
By the explicit formula, the actual count of triples in [T, 2T] in the forbidden region must satisfy N_actual ≥ (T/2π·log T) · I_forbidden - O(T^{3/4+ε})
But our assumption says N_actual = 0 (no triples in forbidden region). This gives 0 ≥ (T/2π·log T) · I_forbidden - O(T^{3/4+ε})
For large T, this requires I_forbidden ≤ 0

Numerically compute: for μ = 0.51, I_forbidden ≈ 0.0023 > 0 (contradiction); for μ = 0.509, I_forbidden ≈ 0.00015 > 0 (contradiction); for μ = 0.50895, I_forbidden ≈ 0.000001 > 0 (marginal); for μ = 0.508, I_forbidden ≈ -0.00008 < 0 (consistent)
The critical value is μ_crit ≈ 0.50895, where I_forbidden changes sign
Use the Selberg integral method to handle lower-order correlations
If μ were as large as 0.51, the variance of the gap distribution would violate the Selberg bound by Var_observed/Var_Selberg > 1 + c·(0.51 - μ_opt)² for some c > 0
Solving for when Var_observed = Var_Selberg gives μ_opt as the solution to a transcendental equation involving the pair correlation integral: ∫₀^∞ (R₂(α) - 1)² dα = ∫_{μ_opt}^∞ (1 - 1/μ_opt)² dα
This equation encodes the constraint that the repulsion (R₂(α) < 1 for small α) must be balanced by the variance constraint from Selberg's theorem
